text: Can you explain everything about the microcontroller to me

A microcontroller is a small computer on a chip that can execute a simple program. It must be connected to a positive and negative pole and can output a voltage of 3 V at its pins, which it can switch on and off (“HIGH” or “LOW”). This switching is controlled by a program, for example with Blockly or in the C++ programming language.
